title: cinchona scrobiculata
intro: Cinchona scrobiculata, also known as the Peruvian cinchona, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Native to the Andean region of South America, this plant is a source of quinine, a natural alkaloid with antimicrobial and antimalarial properties. The medicinal properties of Cinchona scrobiculata have been used to treat malaria, as well as other health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Cinchona scrobiculata include extracts and infusions that can be used to promote overall health and well-being, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
